title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
JavaScript provides several loop constructs:

• for — classic; best when you need the index or a specific count.
• while — repeats while condition is true; good when iterations are unknown.
• do...while — executes body at least once, then checks condition.
• for...of — iterates over iterable values (arrays, strings, maps, sets). Use for arrays.
• for...in — iterates over object keys (enumerable properties). Use for objects, not arrays.
• Array methods (forEach, map, filter) — functional alternatives; often cleaner for collections.

breakexits a loop early. continue skips the rest of the current iteration.
